stimulus.content: Southern colonies grew cotton and shipped it to England. English factories turned the cotton into cloth. Then English merchants sold the cloth back to colonists in all three regions. Neither side could easily do without the other.

stem: This passage shows that the colonies and Britain both depended on each other. What is this called?
answer_explanation: Economic interdependence means that two groups depend on each other for goods and services. The colonies needed British factories to turn raw cotton into cloth, and Britain needed colonial raw materials. This mutual reliance is the definition of economic interdependence.
